mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate signals improving labor market conditions and rising household disposable income, which drives consumer spending and lifts corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE drops below 9.0% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: The joint acceleration of housing starts and retail sales growth indicates synchronized strength in durable goods consumption and construction activity, which amplifies corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term (HOUST growth × RSAFS growth) shows a negative or statistically insignificant coefficient in a multivariate regression of 20-day SPX returns over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the industrial production index reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and higher output utilization, which directly boosts corporate earnings and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are uncorrelated with INDPRO growth rates (correlation coefficient < 0.1) across three consecutive economic cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Sequential quarterly growth in real GDP signals broad-based economic expansion, which improves corporate cash flow projections and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window as institutional portfolios rebalance.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 growth versus 5-day SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the CPI exceeding 0.5% triggers inflation hedging flows and anticipates tighter monetary policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL MoM exceeds 0.5% over a 4-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of historical instances over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts exceeding 700 thousand units signals strong builder confidence and future construction activity, which lifts financials and materials sector earnings and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no statistically significant difference (p > 0.10) between periods where HOUST exceeds 700 versus periods where it remains below.
